extra.reasoning:
Each step event applies acc := (3*acc + c) mod 16, where the nested if-then-else subtracts 16 up to three times — equivalent to mod 16 for all reachable acc in [0,15] (verified: 3*15+13=58<64 so three subtractions always suffice). Constants: step_0 adds 3, step_1 adds 5, step_2 adds 13.

The faulty resume path [t_resume_glitch] fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and acc == 2), setting sent := true — which violates globally(opted_out implies not sent). So the target accumulator value is acc == 2 in the opted-out state.

I did a BFS over the recurrence from the initial acc=0. acc=2 is reachable in 2 steps:
  start acc=0
  step_1: (3*0+5) mod 16 = 5
  step_0: (3*5+3) mod 16 = 18 mod 16 = 2

The optout transition sets opted_out:=true and sent:=false but does NOT modify acc, so acc stays 2. Then send_attempt matches t_resume_glitch (opted_out and acc==2) and sets sent:=true.

Full simulation of [step_1, step_0, optout, send_attempt]:
  step_1 -> opted_out=False, sent=False, acc=5
  step_0 -> opted_out=False, sent=False, acc=2
  optout -> opted_out=True, sent=False, acc=2
  send_attempt -> opted_out=True, sent=True, acc=2  <-- opted_out AND sent both true

This reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating the property. The witness is 4 events, well within the 18-event bound. (acc=2 is genuinely reachable; the set of reachable acc values is {0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7,9,10,12,13,14,15}, so this is not one of the unreachable-target cases.)
